key_points: Statistics is the science of collecting, organizing, presenting, analyzing, and interpreting data.
Frequency: Number of times a particular value occurs.
Cumulative frequency: Sum of frequencies up to a certain class.
Measures of central tendency: Mean, Median, Mode.
Graphical representation: Bar graph, Histogram, Frequency Polygon, Cumulative Frequency Curve.
formulas: Mode (grouped data) = L + [(f1 − f0)/(2f1 − f0 − f2)] × h
Cumulative frequency: Add frequencies sequentially up to a class
Less than cumulative frequency curve: plot upper class boundaries vs cumulative frequencies
examples: Median class: Identify the class containing n/2-th item
Mode: Identify class with highest frequency and apply formula
exam_tips: Always check that frequencies add up to total n.
For median, draw less-than cumulative frequency graph to locate median class.
Mode is applicable for grouped data and requires the modal class.
Mean can also be calculated using assumed mean method for large numbers.
Draw clear graphs (histogram, frequency polygon) to avoid mistakes.
